_comment: ADCS CA Migration Parameters -- Complete this file for your environment
_scenario_help: RootCA | IssuingCA -- Which tier of CA are you migrating?
_option_help: PreDistributed | CrossSigned -- Pre-Distributed: new root distributed before cutover. Cross-Signed: old root cross-signs new root for seamless chain bridging.

oldServer._help: The EXISTING CA server (source). This is the server running the CA you are migrating FROM.
oldServer._caCommonName_help: Optional. Auto-detected from registry. Provide only if the server hosts multiple CAs.

newServer._help: The TARGET CA server. This is the clean server with HSM KSP installed where the new CA will be built.
newServer._vmAdminUsername_help: Local admin username on the NEW VM. Required for Cloud HSM -- certutil -installcert hangs under SYSTEM with Cavium KSP. The orchestrator runs this step as the specified user via scheduled task.

parentCA._help: The ROOT CA that signs the new Issuing CA cert. Required for IssuingCA scenario only. Ignored for RootCA scenario.

newCA._help: Configuration for the new CA being created on the target server.
newCA._caCommonName_help: REQUIRED. Must include a generational suffix (e.g., 'Contoso-RootCA-G2'). Prevents same-CN chain collision when both CAs coexist in trust stores.
newCA._keyAlgorithm_help: RSA | ECDSA_P256 | ECDSA_P384 | ECDSA_P521 -- Determines CryptoProviderName format: RSA uses 'RSA#<KSP>', ECDSA uses 'ECDSA_P256#<KSP>' etc.
newCA._keyLength_help: RSA: 2048 | 3072 | 4096. ECDSA: ignored (auto-derived from keyAlgorithm: P256=256, P384=384, P521=521).
newCA._hashAlgorithm_help: SHA256 | SHA384 | SHA512. SHA256 is recommended for both RSA and ECDSA.
newCA._overwriteExisting_help: Set to true only if Step 2 reported an existing CA configuration.

trust._publishToAD_help: Publish new CA cert to Active Directory via certutil -dspublish. Requires Enterprise Admin.
trust._skipADPublish_help: Skip AD publishing (standalone CAs, lab environments, or manual distribution).

advanced._help: Optional advanced settings. Defaults work for most environments.
